title: anchusa stylosa subsp. spruneri
intro: Anchusa stylosa subsp. spruneri, also known as the Maltese bugloss, is a plant species in the borage family, Boraginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion. The aerial parts of the plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
